segments.text: spake unto Moses
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: saying,
Speak unto all the congregation of the children of Israel, and say unto them, Ye shall be holy: for I the LORD your God am holy.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Ye shall fear every man his mother, and his father, and keep my sabbaths: I am the LORD your God.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Turn ye not unto idols, nor make to yourselves molten gods: I am the LORD your God.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And if ye offer a sacrifice of peace offerings unto the LORD, ye shall offer it at your own will.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: It shall be eaten the same day ye offer it, and on the morrow: and if ought remain until the third day, it shall be burnt in the fire.
And if it be eaten at all on the third day, it is abominable; it shall not be accepted.
Therefore every one that eateth it shall bear his iniquity, because he hath profaned the hallowed thing of the LORD: and that soul shall be cut off from among his people.
And when ye reap the harvest of your land, thou shalt not wholly reap the corners of thy field, neither shalt thou gather the gleanings of thy harvest.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And thou shalt not glean thy vineyard, neither shalt thou gather every grape of thy vineyard; thou shalt leave them for the poor and stranger: I am the LORD your God.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Ye shall not steal, neither deal falsely, neither lie one to another.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And ye shall not swear by my name falsely, neither shalt thou profane the name of thy God: I am the LORD.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Thou shalt not hate thy brother in thine heart: thou shalt in any wise rebuke thy neighbour, and not suffer sin upon him.
segments.speaker: The LORD

footnotes: or, that thou bear not sin for him
heading: Love for Neighbors

segments.text: Thou shalt not avenge, nor bear any grudge against the children of thy people, but thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself: I am the LORD.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And whosoever lieth carnally with a woman, that is a bondmaid, betrothed to an husband, and not at all redeemed, nor freedom given her; she shall be scourged; they shall not be put to death, because she was not free.

footnotes: betrothed…: or, abused by any: Heb. reproached by (or, for) man
she shall…: or, they, etc: Heb. there shall be a scourging
heading: Sexual Conduct

segments.text: But in the fourth year all the fruit thereof shall be holy to praise the LORD withal.

footnotes: holy…: Heb. holiness of praises to the LORD

segments.text: Do not prostitute thy daughter, to cause her to be a whore; lest the land fall to whoredom, and the land become full of wickedness.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And if a stranger sojourn with thee in your land, ye shall not vex him.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Just balances, just weights, a just ephah, and a just hin, shall ye have: I am the LORD your God, which brought you out of the land of Egypt.
segments.speaker: The LORD
